description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration granted approval to pembrolizumab for the treatment of adult and pediatric patients with unresectable or metastatic microsatellite instability-high (MSI-H) or mismatch repair deficient (dMMR) solid tumors, as determined by an FDA approved test, that have progressed following prior treatment and who have no satisfactory alternative treatment options.

contributor.name: Van Allen lab
contributor.description: Van Allen lab, Dana-Farber Cancer Institute

indication.indication: KEYTRUDA is a programmed death receptor-1 (PD-1)-blocking antibody indicated for the treatment of adult and pediatric patients with unresectable or metastatic microsatellite instability-high (MSI-H) or mismatch repair deficient (dMMR) solid tumors, as determined by an FDA approved test, that have progressed following prior treatment and who have no satisfactory alternative treatment options.
indication.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration granted approval to pembrolizumab for the treatment of adult and pediatric patients with unresectable or metastatic microsatellite instability-high (MSI-H) or mismatch repair deficient (dMMR) solid tumors, as determined by an FDA approved test, that have progressed following prior treatment and who have no satisfactory alternative treatment options.
